description_long: This object is a vintage television with a rectangular wooden cabinet and a prominent black screen framed by a thick bezel. The front panel features a speaker grille with a mesh pattern, the brand name 'FAILION', and two rotary control knobs for volume and channel selection. The sides and top are finished in a dark wood grain, giving it a classic, retro appearance. The back of the television is a protruding, boxy section made of black plastic or metal, likely housing the cathode ray tube and ventilation slots. The overall design is bulky and solid, typical of mid-to-late 20th-century televisions. The object appears well-preserved, with clear texturing and defined edges.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a wood-grain cabinet, black screen, and front control knobs, featuring a prominent rear housing.
description_view_0: Front view showing the black screen, speaker grille, brand name 'FAILION', and two rotary control knobs.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the wood-grain finish and the depth of the cabinet, with the control knobs slightly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the protruding black back housing with ventilation slots, contrasting with the wood-grain sides.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the continuous wood-grain finish and the depth of the television, with no controls visible.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, black, matte-finished bezel with rounded corners and a robust, retro style.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of two rotary knobs and a speaker grille on the front right panel.
